key_insight: A 5D PCYS interpolation-table surrogate can emulate VPSC-based irradiation creep/growth in FEM, enabling multi-scale nuclear component simulations at much lower cost.
engineering_relevance: This lets engineers analyze spacer grids and cladding over reactor lifetimes in Code_Aster without the prohibitive runtime of full crystal-plasticity coupling.
method: Build a static 5D interpolation table to represent instantaneous viscoplastic response.
Periodically recalibrate the surrogate with first-order Taylor linearization to track radiation-induced microstructural drift.
Embed the surrogate in Code_Aster and compare against VPSC-FEM benchmarks under high-dose irradiation.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that the linearization scheme keeps relative errors below 1% for representative deformation paths and yields meaningful computational savings in spacer-grid simulations.
watch_item: The approach depends on periodic recalibration and a precomputed table, so accuracy may degrade outside the calibrated irradiation and deformation regimes.
paper_title: Accelerating Multi-scale Simulations of Nuclear Components via PCYS Interpolation Tables
